matching_rule: One base case matches when a promoter or gene-body variant is visible in the current sample. Three additional case families match when the whitelist mean beta, the gene-name-related mean beta, or the all-numeric mean beta resolves to low, medium, or high methylation. Variant prediction rules receive the decoded sample GT, zygosity, ALT dosage, and site REF -> ALT definition; curated allele rules take precedence only when the sample genotype carries the relevant allele dosage.
disclaimer: Predictive theses in this database are literature-guided research summaries derived from the bundled gene interpretation bundle. They are designed for exploratory synthesis in the UI and should not be treated as diagnostic or therapeutic claims.
seeded_markers: rs1837253 (TSLP asthma and allergic-disease regulatory marker)
concrete_variant_prediction: The variant observed in this sample suggests an epithelial TSLP alarmin thesis: the individual may carry a research-grade modifier of TSLP expression, dendritic-cell priming, type 2 inflammation, asthma, atopic dermatitis, or allergic disease context. This does not create a medication-response rule.

variant_prediction_rules.common_name: TSLP asthma and allergic-disease regulatory marker
lookup_keys: TSLP asthma and allergic-disease regulatory marker
variant_prediction_rules.prediction: GT-confirmed rs1837253 dosage suggests this sample carries the TSLP prediction context described by the gene-level thesis. The variant observed in this sample suggests an epithelial TSLP alarmin thesis: the individual may carry a research-grade modifier of TSLP expression, dendritic-cell priming, type 2 inflammation, asthma, atopic dermatitis, or allergic disease context. This does not create a medication-response rule. This specific marker is curated as TSLP asthma and allergic-disease regulatory marker. rs1837253 is bundled as a TSLP research marker for allergic disease, asthma, eczema, airway inflammation, or immune-regulatory cohort literature. Treat it as low-penetrance, cohort-aware association context rather than as a diagnostic or treatment-selection allele.
variant_prediction_rules.sample_change_template: This sample matched rs1837253 at site definition {change} with GT {gt_raw} decoded as {genotype} ({zygosity}). Anchor the TSLP prediction to genotype dosage {allele_dosage} rather than REF -> ALT presence alone. Dosage-specific conclusions should be softened when GT, depth, balance, or genotype quality are uncertain.
variant_prediction_rules.basis: May tag regulatory, expression, coding, haplotype, or linked-locus variation around TSLP.

evidence.label: Ensembl GRCh37 ENSG00000145777: TSLP coordinates and transcripts

methylation_sources.label: Whitelist mean beta
methylation_sources.description: Uses the curated whitelist probes stored in the interpretation database for the current gene.

methylation_sources.label: Gene-name-related mean beta
methylation_sources.description: Uses rows whose gene annotation explicitly names the current gene.

methylation_sources.label: All numeric-row mean beta
methylation_sources.description: Uses every numeric beta value that survived preprocessing for the current sample.

cases.label: Gene variant found
cases.prediction: The variant observed in this sample suggests an epithelial TSLP alarmin thesis: the individual may carry a research-grade modifier of TSLP expression, dendritic-cell priming, type 2 inflammation, asthma, atopic dermatitis, or allergic disease context. This does not create a medication-response rule. An observed TSLP variant places this sample in the curated TSLP research context. The bundled TSLP knowledge base is focused on asthma, allergy, atopic dermatitis, airway inflammation, epithelial-barrier biology, and immune-regulatory research. Seeded markers and locus models are intended as research context and should not be promoted to diagnostic, prognostic, or treatment-selection claims without external clinical curation. TSLP markers are usually interpreted as regulatory modifiers of epithelial cytokine tone and allergic-airway susceptibility.
cases.rationale: This is the base thesis that activates as soon as any promoter or gene-body variant is visible in the current sample.
research_focus: Epithelial alarmin and type 2 inflammation research

cases.label: Gene variant found + high whitelist mean beta
cases.prediction: When a TSLP variant is paired with high methylation in the whitelist mean beta, the sample best fits a combined regulatory-context thesis. This pattern leans toward stronger regulatory restraint or reduced local accessibility around the locus. The variant side of the thesis is: The variant observed in this sample suggests an epithelial TSLP alarmin thesis: the individual may carry a research-grade modifier of TSLP expression, dendritic-cell priming, type 2 inflammation, asthma, atopic dermatitis, or allergic disease context. This does not create a medication-response rule. TSLP methylation should be read as local regulatory context around an allergic-inflammation gene. Promoter-proximal or gene-body beta values may help frame accessibility or expression hypotheses, but they are not validated stand-alone biomarkers for asthma, eczema, allergy, immune activation, or treatment response. The bundled TSLP knowledge base is focused on asthma, allergy, atopic dermatitis, airway inflammation, epithelial-barrier biology, and immune-regulatory research. Seeded markers and locus models are intended as research context and should not be promoted to diagnostic, prognostic, or treatment-selection claims without external clinical curation. TSLP markers are usually interpreted as regulatory modifiers of epithelial cytokine tone and allergic-airway susceptibility.
